nodes.name: ConstructGeometryModel
nodes.description: Create an immutable vehicle geometry/state model from explicit axle-length parameters.
nodes.conceptual_summary: Immutable Asymmetric Offset Configuration. Constructs a canonical configuration object from two non-negative scalar offsets defined on opposite sides of a reference origin. The transform is a direct parameter-to-structure mapping with boundary validation, without optimization, recursion, or stochastic sampling. The resulting artifact is immutable, preserving the input values as fixed geometric/state invariants.. Applications: Robotics: instantiate a rigid-link kinematic segment model from proximal and distal link extents around a joint origin., Materials engineering: define an immutable cross-section descriptor from layer thicknesses on opposite sides of a neutral axis., Computational imaging: encode asymmetric sensor active-region margins (left/right or near/far) into a fixed calibration schema., Neuroscience modeling: represent asymmetric receptive-field spatial extents around a central reference point in a simulation state object.

nodes.name: LoadModelFromFile
nodes.description: Deserialize model geometry parameters from storage into an immutable model spec.
nodes.conceptual_summary: ImmutableStructuredSpecHydration. Accepts a locator to persisted serialized data, reads the byte stream, and decodes it using a predefined schema into a typed in-memory parameter structure. The reconstructed structure is then frozen so downstream computations can rely on referential stability and mutation-free semantics.. Applications: Hydrating finite-element mesh and boundary-condition specifications for mechanical simulation from a persisted artifact., Loading calibrated kinematic-chain parameters for robotic motion planning from a configuration file., Reconstructing neuroimaging preprocessing pipeline parameter sets from stored manifests for reproducible analysis., Restoring compiler optimization-pass configuration graphs from serialized build metadata.

nodes.name: QueryGeometryParameters
nodes.description: Project front length, rear length, and derived wheelbase from the immutable model spec.
nodes.conceptual_summary: Bipartite Scalar Projection with Additive Extent Derivation. The operation reads two non-negative scalar components from an immutable structured input and returns them directly as explicit outputs. It then computes a third scalar by linear addition of those components, producing a total 1D extent. No state is changed, and the mapping is fully deterministic.. Applications: Genomics: expose upstream/downstream sequence segment lengths and derive total amplicon span., Network engineering: expose forward/reverse path propagation delays and derive round-trip baseline delay., Manufacturing process planning: expose pre-process/post-process motion distances and derive total traverse distance., Signal processing: expose pre-event/post-event window durations and derive full analysis window length.

nodes.name: ComputeSideslipAngle
nodes.description: Compute sideslip from steering input and vehicle geometry as a pure kinematic transform.
nodes.conceptual_summary: Kinematic Deflection-to-Drift Angle Projection. Maps a single control deflection angle and fixed rigid-body geometry parameters to an instantaneous orientation offset angle using a closed-form trigonometric relation. The transform is purely kinematic (no forces, no integration over time): it projects local actuator deflection into global trajectory-direction deviation relative to a body-aligned reference axis. Zero input deflection yields zero output offset, and output sign follows input sign under symmetric parameterization.. Applications: Planar mobile robotics: convert steering-joint deflection and platform geometry into trajectory-heading offset for path tracking., Aerospace guidance: map control-surface deflection and airframe geometry to instantaneous flight-path angular deviation in simplified kinematic models., Catheter/continuum-tool navigation: translate proximal angular actuation and segment geometry into tip-direction offset estimates., Computer graphics/animation kinematics: derive motion-direction offset from joint rotation and rig geometry for procedural locomotion.

nodes.name: ComputeLinearizedStateMatrices
nodes.description: Compute linearized system matrices for local dynamics around state/control operating point.
nodes.type_signature: (model_spec: ModelSpec, x: StateVector, u: ControlVector) -> tuple[Matrix, Matrix]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Local First-Order Sensitivity Linearization. Given a nonlinear vector-valued transition map and a selected operating point, this atom computes the first-order partial derivatives with respect to the internal configuration vector and the external excitation vector. The result is a tangent-space approximation that maps small perturbations to first-order output changes. This converts a locally nonlinear update rule into a pair of linear operators suitable for analysis, prediction, and optimization.. Applications: Linearizing nonlinear biochemical reaction kinetics around a concentration operating point for stability and control of synthetic pathways, Approximating local behavior of electric power-network dynamics around a load-generation equilibrium for small-signal stability studies, Deriving local sensitivity operators in climate submodels (e.g., coupled temperature-moisture fields) for data assimilation and forecast correction, Building local tangent models of epidemiological compartment transitions under intervention inputs for short-horizon policy optimization

nodes.name: DeriveOperatingPointGeometry
nodes.description: Compute shared geometric/kinematic terms at the operating point (sideslip, wheelbase, and combined heading) used by both Jacobians.

inputs.name: model_spec
inputs.constraints: Provides wheelbase and rear-length parameters; deterministic sideslip mapping.

inputs.name: u
inputs.constraints: u[0] is longitudinal velocity, u[1] is steering angle.

nodes.type_signature: (model_spec: ModelSpec, x: StateVector<3>, u: ControlVector<2>) -> tuple[f64, f64, f64]

nodes.name: LinearizeStateDynamicsWrtState
nodes.description: Evaluate the state Jacobian A = df/dx for the kinematic model, using the known sparse structure with only yaw-coupled nonzero terms.

outputs.name: A
outputs.constraints: Rows/cols follow state ordering [x, y, yaw].

nodes.name: LinearizeDynamicsWrtVelocityInput
nodes.description: Compute the velocity-input sensitivity column (df/du0), including translational terms and yaw-rate term normalization.

inputs.name: u
inputs.constraints: Finite; steering not at singular tan/cos points.

nodes.type_signature: (model_spec: ModelSpec, u: ControlVector<2>, yaw_plus_sideslip: f64, wheelbase_total: f64) -> Vector<3>

nodes.name: LinearizeDynamicsWrtSteeringInput
nodes.description: Compute the steering-input sensitivity column (df/du1), including sideslip derivative propagation and yaw-rate derivative rational term.
nodes.type_signature: (model_spec: ModelSpec, u: ControlVector<2>, yaw_plus_sideslip: f64, wheelbase_total: f64) -> Vector<3>

nodes.name: AssembleControlJacobian
nodes.description: Assemble control Jacobian B by concatenating velocity and steering sensitivity columns in control order.

nodes.name: EvaluateAndInvertDynamics
nodes.description: Evaluate nonlinear derivatives, compute Jacobian at time t, and solve inverse-input mapping as pure transforms.
nodes.type_signature: (model_spec: ModelSpec, x: StateVector, u: ControlVector, _t: float, _x_dot: StateDerivativeVector) -> tuple[StateDerivativeVector, Matrix, ControlVector]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Bidirectional Nonlinear State-Transition Operator. Applies a parameterized nonlinear map to a state-control tuple at a given scalar index to produce an instantaneous state-rate vector. Simultaneously computes the local first-order sensitivity matrix of that map with respect to the state, yielding a local linearization around the evaluation point. It also solves the inverse actuation problem: given state, state-rate, and index, it infers a control vector consistent with the same dynamics under local invertibility assumptions.. Applications: Robotic manipulation: map joint configuration and actuator commands to joint-rate dynamics, estimate sensitivity, and recover required actuator signals for a target motion derivative, Systems biology: map concentration-state and intervention vector to reaction-rate derivatives, compute local sensitivity matrix, and infer intervention levels to realize desired rate profiles, Electric power systems: map grid state and control injections to state-derivative evolution, derive local Jacobian for stability analysis, and infer control injections for target transient behavior, Aerospace guidance: map vehicle state and command inputs to kinematic derivatives, compute local Jacobian for trajectory sensitivity, and infer commands that produce required derivative constraints

nodes.name: DeriveSlipAdjustedKinematics
nodes.description: Compute sideslip from steering and derive shared geometric terms for the bicycle-model dynamics.

inputs.name: x
inputs.constraints: x[2] is yaw angle in radians.

inputs.name: u
inputs.constraints: u[1] is steering/road wheel angle in radians.

nodes.type_signature: (model_spec: ModelSpec, x: StateVector (na::SVector<f64, 3>), u: ControlVector (na::SVector<f64, 2>)) -> tuple[f64, f64, f64]

nodes.name: EvaluateNonlinearStateDerivative
nodes.description: Evaluate planar position and yaw derivatives using speed, steering, sideslip, and wheelbase.
nodes.type_signature: (u: ControlVector (na::SVector<f64, 2>), sideslip: f64, effective_heading: f64, wheelbase: f64) -> StateDerivativeVector (na::SVector<f64, 3>)

nodes.name: LinearizeDynamicsAtOperatingPoint
nodes.description: Compute local Jacobian matrices with respect to state and control at (x, u).

inputs.name: _t
inputs.constraints: Interface parameter; unused in current implementation.

nodes.type_signature: (model_spec: ModelSpec, x: StateVector (na::SVector<f64, 3>), u: ControlVector (na::SVector<f64, 2>), _t: f64) -> (na::SMatrix<f64, 3, 3>, na::SMatrix<f64, 3, 2>)

nodes.name: InferControlFromStateRate
nodes.description: Map current state and desired state derivative to inferred control via inverse dynamics interface.

inputs.name: _t
inputs.constraints: Interface parameter; currently unused by placeholder.

nodes.type_signature: (model_spec: ModelSpec, x: StateVector (na::SVector<f64, 3>), _x_dot: StateDerivativeVector (na::SVector<f64, 3>), _t: f64) -> ControlVector (na::SVector<f64, 2>)
